knimeNode.name: Excel Reader (non-tabular)
knimeNode.shortDescription: Reads non-tabular, form-structured Excel worksheets into a KNIME data table using a configurable field mapping definition.

fullDescription.intro: Reads one or more .xlsx files containing form-structured (non-tabular) worksheets and extracts field values into a standard KNIME data table. The form structure is defined by a connected input table that maps field names to Excel cell addresses (e.g. C4, B10:D15). Each worksheet produces one output row in wide mode, or one row per field in long mode. Supports single file, all sheets in a workbook, folder, and recursive folder reading modes.
fullDescription.option: Select Single File to read from one .xlsx file, or Folder to read all .xlsx files in a directory.
The path to the .xlsx file or the folder containing .xlsx files to be read.
When reading in Folder mode, also scan all subfolders recursively for .xlsx files.
The name of the worksheet to read from each workbook. Leave blank to read the first sheet.
Comma-separated list of worksheet names to skip when reading all sheets from a workbook. Default: Config.

Wide: produces one row per form instance (file/sheet pair), with one column per field defined in the mapping table. Long: produces one row per field per form instance, with fixed columns field_name and value.

option.name: Add Source File and Sheet Name Columns

fullDescription.option: When enabled, adds source_file and sheet_name as the first two columns in the output table, identifying the origin of each row.
When a Cell Range address specifies a range (e.g. B10:D15), all cell values in the range are concatenated using this delimiter string.

When enabled, label fields (Content Type = "label" in the form definition table) are included in the main output table alongside data fields, preserving the definition table order. When disabled, only data fields appear in port 0.

When enabled, label fields are extracted and output to port 1 as a separate table with fixed columns: Name, Cell Range, and Cell Content (the extracted value), plus optional provenance columns. When disabled, port 1 produces an empty table.
Warn and set missing value: if a cell address cannot be resolved, a missing value is written and a warning is logged. Fail: the node fails immediately on the first unresolvable cell.

Warn and set missing value: if a cell value cannot be converted to the declared data type, a missing value is written and a warning is logged. Fail: the node fails immediately on the first conversion error.

ports.inPort: A table defining the form structure mapping field names to Excel cell addresses. Required columns: Name (String) — the output column name; Cell Range (String) — the Excel cell address (e.g. C4) or range (e.g. B10:D15). Optional columns: Content Type (String) — the field role, either "data" (default) or "label"; Data Type (String) — the expected value type, one of: string, int, double, date, boolean (defaults to string if absent or blank).
ports.outPort: The extracted form data as a flat KNIME table. When provenance columns are enabled, source_file (String) and sheet_name (String) are always the first two columns, identifying the origin of each row. In wide mode: one row per (file, sheet) pair, one column per field. In long mode: one row per (file, sheet, field) triple, with fixed columns field_name (String) and value (String).

Label field values extracted from the form. One row per label field per (file, sheet) pair. Columns: Source File (optional), Sheet Name (optional), Name, Cell Range, Cell Content. Only populated when "Output label fields in port 1" is enabled in the General tab. Empty table otherwise.
